Remarks: The Finance Enterprise Group for the transaction
A user-defined unique identifier used to group together a set of records to process selectively or concurrently.
A user-defined unique identifier assigned to each transaction to be transferred into the CSF system. If all other key fields are alike, this may be used so that no duplicates will occur.
This is the accounting entity that is originating the entry.
ĀValid values are:
0-Unreleased
1-Error
2-Budget Edit
ĀAn optional user-defined field that contains the CSF Accounting Entity and accounting unit. This field determines what accounting entity and accounting unit a record will convert to.
The to accounting entity the transaction will post to
The accounting unit the transaction will post to
This field can be used 2 ways - one is as a user-defined field that will crosswalk the account to the CSF account. It can also be the actual account to be posted to.
ĀUsed to indicate where the transaction was created.
TC will default if this field is left blank.
ĀValid values are:
YYYYMMDD
ĀThe reference number associated with the transaction.
ĀUser-defined description of the transaction record
ĀUser-defined currency code used if the transaction is not in the accounting entity's base currency.
ĀContains the transaction units amount.
ĀTransaction Amount (positive or negative) in currency to be posted
ĀA two-character code representing an application used within the CSF system (for example, GL=Global Ledger, AP=Payables).
GL will default if left blank
ĀAuto reversal is the process of reversing the transaction. If you select auto reverse, Period Closing (GL199) creates a reversing journal entry in the next period for this transaction.
ĀThis date, assigned to the journal entry, determines what period and year the transaction (journal entry) will reside in after completing the Transaction Conversion process.
Valid values are:
YYYYMMDD
The project the transaction will post to
ĀThe core ledger will default if an alternative ledger is not selected
ĀA reference field used for sub-system journaling by document. If the company you are converting to is journaling by document, this field will be used to determine where to batch a new journal entry.
Indicates the error message, system generated.
If the transaction is auto reversing, enter the date the reverse should occur.  If this field is left blank, it will reverse the next month.
If a recourse is assigned to this transaction, enter it here.
If a vendor is to be attached to the transaction, the vendor group for the vendor needs to be entered here
If a vendor is to be attached to the transaction, enter the vendor number here
If the transaction is to be billed, enter the status of the billing.  Will default to Unprocessed if left blank
Valid values are:
0-Unprocessed
1-Invoiced
3-Processed
4-Distributed
If the transaction is to have the revenue recognized, enter the status of the revenue recognition.  Will defualt to No if left blank
Valid values are:
0-No
1-Yes
2-Hold
If the transaction is to be capitalized, enter the status.
Valid values are:
0-Unprocessed
1-Processed
ĀIf the labor will be distributed, enter the status.
Valid values are:
0-Unprocessed
1-Processed
2-Distributed
